title: rubus incisus var. subcrataegifolius
intro: Rubus incisus var. subcrataegifolius, also known as the thimbleberry, is a rare and lesser-known variety of bramble native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. The plant's medicinal properties include its use in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, due to its anthraquinone content. The health benefits of Rubus incisus var. subcrataegifolius include its potential to lower blood sugar levels and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a promising natural remedy for diabetes and inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties, although caution should be exercised due to its potential toxicity and side effects.
